entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements List - Add and Implement Action Button
entry.content:
Hi Pawan,

Thank you so much for excellent blog and it works perfectly for update Scenarios.

Did you get chance to post blog for download functionality. I have requirement to download TXT file from Fiori Element App.

with this approach button showing when ROW is selected but I want button should enable  by default.

Much Appreciated if you share the process add button to  download TXT file.

entry.title: Job cancelled without reason
entry.content:
Hello community,

I have a specific program that runs in the background via a job, it is a process that takes several hours for an initialization phase, on average 16-18 hours (a few minutes later), several tests have been successfully performed for this initialization.

But for a few days new tests have been launched on the qualification server and the job is canceled by the system after about 8 hours of execution, no dump from the program (ST22).

The job logs say to go see the system logs (SM21); this is very system information, which I do not really know how to analyze.

Here is the data that I have in my possession from the Syslog log details view:

Message details
Message ID           Q02
Category             Operation trace
Message Text         Stops work process 43 (PID = 52430, Info = Exit with status 0)

Session details
Package              STSK

Technical details
SysLog type          m Error (Function,Module,Row)
variable data        wp_halthalt wpdpuxtool 334

Parameter details
Message
Function             wp_halt
Reason               halt wp
Program              dpuxtool
Line                  334

What can I do at my level as a developer? Should I check memory consumption?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: NOW RELEASED: Upload and work with external files in SAP Cloud ALM for implementation
entry.content: Is it working with Google doc ?

entry.title: System Creating Reservation instead of PO
entry.content:
Hello All Experts, 

One question, currently for Network-Materials system is creating Reservation instead of PO. 

What settings/configuration I need to do or required, so that system will start creating PO and not Reservation. 

Business wants PO to be created for Network-Material. 

Expert's guidance will be appreciated. 

Thank you. 

Regards,

Tanuj K.

entry.title: Update processing status field of standard CDS I_purchaseorderapi01 using API or EML
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

I am working on workflow development in BTP-SBPA. There is a requirement to update the status of PO (PurchaseProcessingStatus) in CDS I_purchaseOrderApi01 based on workflow result. I have tried using standard API for purchase order but status field is Read-only. Is there any another way to update the status ?

Backend system is S4HANA Public cloud. 

Thanks in advance!

ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

entry.title: Return Process during PGR Required Movement Type 655 Goods Returns QI (Quality Inspection) Required.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We need your help on this specific return process. Our business requirement is that during PGR goods will move to quality inspection. I have checked through the order type CBAR standard return process, where I am getting only 653 and 657 movement types triggered automatically as per follow-up activity. And in the Lean Return CBRE, only 651 and 653 are available to determine. As you knew, we can't create any schedule lines in the public cloud. In this case, I need your support; how could I achieve my client requirements?

Client wants during outbound delivery for returns. Order Specific Goods should be under Quality Inspection, with below points

  1. QC blocked the FG item where it needed rework to ensure the required quality.
  2. Customer returned goods are sorted and reworked for ensuring the required quality of FG items.

Please Guide us How could we achieve it? 

Thanks
Hasnat

entry.title: Re: How to control pre-defined asset class
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please read the following information sources:

Using Configuration Options

Please note that you can leave your feedback using the "Feedback" button.

3095410 - Edit standard Asset Classes in SSCUI - S/4HANA CLOUD

SAP S/4HANA Cloud : Asset Accounting - FAQs (2022)

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Activate Job Template in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Here is how to generalize your blog post to any standard Fiori App for which you don't see a Job Template proposed:

  1. Obtain the ID of the Fiori App via Profile button > About (e.g. F2823 is obtained in the case of "Product Classification for Demand-Driven Replenishment")

  2. Search the Fiori App ID in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (the following page is obtained in the case of F2823: Schedule Product Classification (DD))
  3. Go to the panel IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION > Configuration, e.g.:
  4. Get the value of the parameter "JobCatalogEntryName" in the App Launcher, e.g.:
  5. Now you know the value to add to the SPRO activity "Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalog Entries and Maintain your Job Catalog" or more directly SM30 APJ_C_SCOPE, e.g. "SAP_SCM_DD_PRODUCT_CLASSFCTN" in the case of F2823:

entry.title: Re: How to deactivate cost center for cost element
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: User Replication from Cloud Identity Service to SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Pre-requisite: You should have an SAP BTP account and should have cloud Identity Service enabled.

Step 1: Log into your Cloud Identity Services account

Goto Identity Provisioning -> Source Systems

Step 2: Click on Add to add a new source system

Step 3: Goto Properties section and maintain the properties as shown below.

URL should be the cloud identity service URL.

Then Click on Save

Step 4: Goto Outbound Certificates Tab and then download the certificate using the download button as shown below

Step 5: Goto Users & Authorizations Tab and Navigate to Administrators.

Step 6: Click on Add -> System as administrator. Enter a system name and configure the authorizations by Turning on/off, then Click on Save

 

 

 

 

After saving, you should be able to see an option “Configure System Authentication”

Goto to Certificate and drilldown and upload the certificate which was downloaded from step 4 while configuring source systems and save it.

 

 

Step 7: Goto Identity Provisioning and select Target Systems

Click on “Add” to add a new Target system to which the users need to be replicated.

Type – SAP BTP ABAP environment

Source system:  Pick the source system which was created in Step 2

 

 

Goto Properties section and maintain the below details and the URL should be the BTP ABAP environment to which users are to be replicated.

 

 

 

 

 

Step 8:  Goto Outbound certificates and use the download option to download the certificate of Target system

 

 

 

Create a communication Arrangement in the Target ABAP system

Login into SAP BTP ABAP system

Step 9:  Goto Communication Management -> Maintain Communication Users

Create a communication user by maintaining User name and Description

And then Upload the Certificate which was downloaded for Target Systems at Step 8

 

 

Step 10: Create a communication system with system ID and System name and mark it as Inbound only

 

 

Navigate to Users for Inbound communication and add the communication user created above with Authentication method as SSL Client Certificate

 

 

Step 11:  Create a communication Arrangement using communication scenario SAP_COM_0193, select the communication system which was created above.

 

 

You can now go back to Cloud Identity service for replicating the users.

Goto identity Provisioning -> Select the Source System and then go to “Jobs” section

Click on Run and it would replicate the users from your Cloud Identity Service to SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

entry.title: Re: ⚡ Vamos realizar 1º SAP Meet-Up do ano de 2025!
entry.content: Muito bom ver todos vocês reunidos! Abraço a todos!

entry.title: The Future of Enterprise AI: Why SAP is Ahead of the Curve
entry.content:
We at SAP in 2025 have started a mission of helping the customer run better and bring their best in their enterprise architecture. And to achieve this, we know AI-First has to be one of the MANTRAs we need to have as the DNA of every customer's Transformation Enterprise Architecture journey. While we at SAP are making sure, we make AI as an integral part of our every business application and even every service of BTP, question comes how is SAP placed compared to its competitors to venture into such a transformational journey. This article touches upon few facts (with examples) to explain where does SAP stand. 

Please bear it in mind, whatever has been mentioned here, is a personal opinion only based on the available facts.  

SAP has a strong upper hand in the enterprise industry when it comes to an AI-first approach due to its unique capabilities, integrated ecosystem, inhouse enterprise data and industry-specific solutions. Here’s why SAP stands out compared to other enterprise solution providers:

1. Embedded AI in Core Business Processes

SAP integrates AI directly into its core applications, enabling businesses to leverage AI without complex implementations.

Example:

  • SAP S/4HANA: AI is embedded in processes like predictive analytics, demand forecasting, and cash flow management.
    • Example: AI predicts inventory needs based on historical sales data, reducing stockouts and overstocking.

2. Industry-Specific AI Solutions

SAP tailors AI solutions to specific industries, ensuring relevance and effectiveness.

Example:

  • SAP for Retail: AI-driven customer insights and personalized marketing.
    • Example: AI analyzes customer behavior to recommend products, increasing sales.
  • SAP for Manufacturing: Predictive maintenance and quality control.
    • Example: AI predicts machine failures, reducing downtime and maintenance costs.

3. Pre-Built AI Models with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

SAP BTP offers pre-built AI models and tools, enabling faster deployment and reduced development time.

Example:

  • SAP AI Business Services: Ready-to-use AI services like invoice processing and sentiment analysis.
    • Example: AI automatically extracts data from invoices, reducing manual effort.

4. Seamless Data Integration

SAP’s AI solutions leverage its vast ecosystem, ensuring seamless data integration across modules and systems.

Example:

  • SAP Integrated Planning: AI integrates data from SAP ERP, CRM, and supply chain systems for real-time insights.
    • Example: AI optimizes supply chain planning by analyzing data from multiple sources.

5. Focus on Ethical and Responsible AI

SAP emphasizes ethical AI practices, ensuring transparency, fairness, and compliance.

Example:

  • SAP AI Ethics Policy: Ensures AI models are free from bias and adhere to regulatory standards.
    • Example: AI-driven hiring tools in SAP SuccessFactors are designed to avoid bias in candidate selection.

6. Partnerships and Ecosystem

SAP collaborates with leading AI providers like Google, Microsoft, and IBM to enhance its capabilities.

Example:

  • SAP and Google Cloud: Integration of Google’s AI/ML tools with SAP applications.
    • Example: AI-powered demand forecasting using Google’s machine learning models.

7. Real-World Success Stories

SAP’s AI-first approach has delivered measurable results for enterprises.

Example:

  • A renownedbeauty,makeup company used SAP AI to personalize customer experiences, resulting in a 20% increase in sales.
  • A multinational Brewing company leveraged SAP AI for demand forecasting, reducing inventory costs by 15%.

Why SAP is Better Than Other Providers

  1. Holistic Approach: SAP integrates AI across all business functions, not just specific areas.
  2. Industry Expertise: SAP’s deep industry knowledge ensures AI solutions are tailored to specific needs.
  3. Ease of Use: Pre-built AI models and embedded AI reduce complexity and implementation time.
  4. Scalability: SAP’s solutions are designed to scale with growing business needs.
  5. Trust and Compliance: SAP’s focus on ethical AI builds trust and ensures regulatory compliance.

Conclusion

SAP’s AI-first approach is a game-changer for enterprises, offering integrated, industry-specific, and ethical AI solutions that drive real business value. Its ability to seamlessly embed AI into core processes and leverage its vast ecosystem gives it a clear edge over other enterprise solution providers.

entry.title: is batch Odata API request supported in SuccessFactors Learning?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We want to know whether the Batch Operations explained in Batch Operations | SAP Help Portal works for SuccessFactors Learning or not.

If this is possible, could you please explain how to test it for the below call to find the curriculum status of a user. Our goal is to use see if we can pass multiple learning api request in a single go.

/learning/odatav4/public/admin/curriculum-service/v1/UserCurriculumStatuses?$filter=criteria/userID eq '98765' and criteria/curriculumID eq 'curricula1'

entry.content:
Did you actually generate the time dependency, during creation : Environment / Time-Dep./ Generate or was it automatically generated?
Do you see the delimit button?

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Paris #7
entry.content:
Le SAP Community Meetup est de retour à Paris avec un format plus léger et convivial !

Rejoignez-nous pour une soirée d’échanges, de collaboration et de networking autour de SAP. Une belle opportunité pour partager vos expériences, discuter des dernières tendances et passer un bon moment entre passionnés.

Ne manquez pas cette occasion unique de (re)connecter avec la communauté !

À très vite !

entry.title: Re: SELECT all rows except those with computed values
entry.content:
I do not think there is a direct way, but you could construct the query and then run it.  You can use the LIST method to create the select list.  Here is an example (rough implementation but shows the idea).

create or replace table t (
	id bigint not null default autoincrement,
	c1 bigint null default 'test',
	c2 bigint null compute( 5* c1 ),
	primary key ( id asc )
);
insert into t( c1 ) values ( 5 );
select * from t;

create or replace procedure runQueryWithSkipComputedCol( in @ownername char(128), in @tablename char(128) )
begin
    declare @stmt long varchar;

    select
        'select ' +
        list( column_name, ' ,' ) + ' ' + ' from ' +
        su.user_name + '.' +
        st.table_name
    into
        @stmt
    from 
        systabcol stc join 
            systable st join 
                sysuser su 
    where
            su.user_name = @ownername
        and st.table_name = @tablename
        and stc.column_type <> 'c'
    group by st.table_name, su.user_name;
    execute immediate with result set on @stmt;
end;

call runQueryWithSkipComputedCol( 'dba', 't' );

entry.title: Re: Datum field gets changed when getting data from database into itab
entry.content: Use the conversion exit:

entry.title: Re: Enquiry on SQL Anywhere 17.0.10.6089: "SAP JRE" JRE18...
entry.content: I am can only direct you to the KBA - I guess it requires a s-account to access. In summary, the KBA is listing CVEs that scanners will report as issues with the SQL Anywhere shipped JRE. SQL Anywhere use of the JRE are not at risk to those CVEs. But we did update the JRE as part of a periodic refresh of their party components.

Houda.

entry.title: Re: Dressing up your VCAL in LMS
entry.content:
Good morning!

I first want to say thank you so much for this blog post. This was extremely helpful in learning how to customize our VCAL in SAP. 

I did have a question that hopefully the OP or the community can help with.

Currently, under the Global Application Settings, our emails are set up to send the Vcalendar Attachments only (registration confirmation email sends with .ics attachments included). With these current settings, my custom VCAL HTML code works great. No issues there.



We are considering starting to update our settings to enable the second setting, 'Send VCalendar attachments as Calendar Invite'. In testing, when I have this setting enabled, my custom VCAL HTML code does not work. The VCAL sends as the calendar invite instead of the .ics attachments as expected, but the VCAL calendar invite that sends is just blank. None of my customizations or anything populates. 

Does the VCAL handle things differently depending on if the .ics attachments versus sent as calendar invite is used? I'm confused as to why it works great as an .ics attachment but doesn't work at all as a calendar invite.

With the send as calendar invite setting enabled, I was able to remove the HTML and send just a plain text description - this works fine, but I would like to use the HTML code. I just wasn't sure if that was even a possibility or if maybe there is something else I need to change in my code now that the setting has changed? 

Thanks in advance for all the help!

entry.title: Is it a best practice to implement LMS in two instance landscape on global EC which has multiple
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Is it a best practice to implement SuccessFactors LMS in two instance landscape on global client with Employee Central, which has multiple instances running in parallel with over 50,000 users. 

Is that Dev/Stage and Prod landscape a best practice for implementation or should it always be two landscape if the customer is not implementing validated LMS.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Schedule and monitor application related jobs
entry.content:
For information, the descriptions of the options of the tab HEADER originate from the SAP Help Portal > Home > SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP 7.52SAP Fiori Apps for SAP NetWeaver > SAP Fiori Applications > Application Jobs > Defining Job Catalog Entries in ABAP > Parameters on the Header Tab.

Concerning the option "Scoping relevant" (customizing in the table APJ_C_SCOPE), the maintenance is to be done via the transaction code SM30 > APJ_C_SCOPE > Maintain.

Also, the SPRO activity can be reached via SM30 > APJ_C_SCOPE > Customizing > Project OR Continue w/o Specifying Project.

The mention "tuple" is not very clear, it just means that you have to maintain the table APJ_C_SCOPE in each client where you want to run the Fiori App which is using the Job Catalog Entry.

One example of concerned Fiori App is "Product Classification for Demand-Driven Replenishment" (F2823), which is using the job template SAP_SCM_DD_PRODUCT_CLASSFCTN. The maintenance of APJ_C_SCOPE is explained in this blog post: Activate Job Template in S/4 HANA - SAP Community (I also added a comment to generalize the blog post to find out the name of the Job Catalog Entry used by any Fiori App).

entry.title: Issue in previewing application in ADT
entry.content:
Hi community,

Recently I am having previewing List Report applications from ADT. The environment is BTP ABAP environment in trial account.

When I launch an application, it loads, but buttons such as 'Go' do not respond.  In the console, I see several errors as shown below:

When I run the application from BAS, it works as expected. However, when I launch the same app from the HTML5 Application repository, the same issue arises. I hope this is a temporary issue and fil be fixed soon.

entry.content:
• Public Sector

    We have funds center, which have 2 analysis times:

    01012023-31122025 and 01012026-31129999 with different names.

    But when we run FM2G for 2025 year and 2026 year there is the same name from 01012023-31122025 period.

    Do you know why this weird case is?

     

    thanks.

    Best Regards,

    Hanna

     

     

     

     

    •

entry.title: Re: How to clean a GRC obsolete data (Access Requests, EA...
entry.content:
I´ve found the solution myself after some researches:

Through SARA tcode, you can write a Archive of everything by selecting the archive object GRFNMSP and them select to delete it in SARA itself.

Now my GRC is empty of old/obsolete requests.

entry.title: BTP application writing to on-premise HANA database table / Datasphere HANA database table
entry.content:
Hello,

We have some BTP developers creating an app that would write to an on-premise HANA database table (this database is used in for BW/4HANA).

We eventually want to migrate the BTP app to write to a Datasphere table. 

Is there a connection framework where we can easily switch the BTP app from writing to the on-premise HANA database table to writing to the Datasphere table?

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

entry.title: Access the HANA database attached to a S/4HANA on-cloud
entry.content:
I know that we can access the HANA database attached to a S/4HANA on-premise, but is it possible to access the HANA database attached to a S/4HANA on-cloud?

Would there be a difference between the Private and Public Editions of S/4HANA on-cloud

We want to connect Datasphere to the HANA database on a S/4HANA on-cloud.

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

entry.title: SAP Callidus
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

Over the past 5 years I have come across the term "SAP Callidus" in lot of Job Descriptions.
I believe when we say "SAP Callidus"  we are referring to a suite of products that SAP acquired from Callidus Cloud.
Like Callidus TrueComp/CallidusCloud Commissions now known as SAP Commissions
Callidus TrueProducer/CallidusCloud Producer Pro now know as SAP Agent Lifecycle Management
Callidus CPQ now know as SAP CPQ
Callidus Territory and Quota now known as SAP Territory and Quota
Litmos later SAP Litmos (now not a part of SAP)

Is the above the correct understanding or are we referring to a single product as "SAP Callidus".
Some call now SAP Commissions as SAP Callidus 

I basically want to understand if SAP Callidus is used to refer a single product or suite of products.

Please correct me if any of the above is incorrect.

entry.title: Overcoming Challenges in Machine Learning with SAP Solutions
entry.content:
Machine learning (ML) is no longer just a futuristic buzzword—it’s here, transforming industries and reshaping enterprise software solutions. When paired with SAP systems, particularly SAP S/4HANA, machine learning has the potential to unlock powerful insights, streamline processes, and revolutionize how businesses operate. However, integrating machine learning with SAP solutions comes with its fair share of challenges.

Understanding Machine Learning Integration in SAP Solutions

The Role of Machine Learning in SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA, a leading ERP solution, has embedded machine learning capabilities that allow businesses to:

  • Analyze vast datasets in real time.
  • Make predictive decisions across financials, supply chain, and customer relationship management.
  • Automate repetitive tasks, freeing up time for strategic activities.

Drawing from our experience, the integration of ML within SAP S/4HANA is a game-changer for organizations seeking a data-driven competitive edge.

Overview of Embedded Machine Learning Capabilities

SAP systems come equipped with tools like SAP AI Core and SAP AI Business Services, which include pre-built machine learning models for:

  • Invoice matching in finance.
  • Demand forecasting in supply chain.
  • Sentiment analysis in customer service.

After trying out these tools, our findings show that businesses can accelerate their ML adoption without building algorithms from scratch.

Benefits of Integrating Machine Learning with ERP Systems

When machine learning meets ERP systems like SAP, the results are impactful:

  • Improved decision-making: Predictive analytics help businesses anticipate market trends.
  • Cost efficiency: Automation reduces manual workloads and minimizes errors.
  • Enhanced customer satisfaction: Personalized recommendations and faster issue resolutions improve the customer experience.

For instance, companies like Unilever leverage SAP’s ML features to optimize inventory and reduce waste in their supply chain.

Challenges in Implementing Machine Learning

While machine learning offers immense potential, implementation in SAP environments is not without challenges.

Identifying Common Obstacles Faced During Integration

  1. Data Quality Issues:
    Poor data quality or fragmented datasets can lead to inaccurate predictions.
    • Real-life example: A retail company using SAP faced inaccurate forecasting because historical sales data was incomplete.
  2. Compliance and Security Risks:
    Handling sensitive data (e.g., financial or customer data) requires robust security measures to meet regulations like GDPR.
  3. Skill Gaps in Teams:
    Many teams lack the expertise to effectively implement and maintain ML models in SAP systems.
  4. Integration Complexity:
    Existing systems often require significant customization to work with ML algorithms.

Strategies to Overcome Data Quality and Compliance Issues

  • Data Quality:
    Invest in data cleansing tools like SAP Data Intelligence to integrate, validate, and enrich your datasets.
    • Our team discovered through using SAP Data Intelligence that it significantly improved ML model accuracy.
  • Compliance:
    Use built-in security features in SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) to protect sensitive information and ensure regulatory compliance.
  • Skill Building:
    Upskill your team with SAP’s online training resources or partner with experienced consultants.

Key Machine Learning Use Cases in SAP

1. Predictive Analytics for Business Forecasting

Machine learning enables predictive modeling for accurate demand forecasting, helping businesses plan inventory, staffing, and production more effectively.

  • Example: After conducting experiments with SAP Predictive Analytics, a manufacturing company reduced overstock costs by 15%.

2. Enhancing Supply Chain Visibility Through AI and ML

Machine learning improves supply chain operations by providing real-time insights into potential disruptions.

  • Case Study: Retail giant Walmart uses SAP ML features to track shipments and optimize delivery routes.

3. Improving Telehealth Operations with Machine Learning

The healthcare industry, particularly telehealth services, is experiencing a revolution through SAP’s machine learning capabilities. Predictive analytics can optimize patient care by forecasting appointment no-shows and enabling better resource allocation. Moreover, natural language processing (NLP) models embedded in SAP Conversational AI can enhance virtual consultations by enabling real-time translation and symptom analysis.

According to Abto Software, telehealth consultations increased by 154% during the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting the importance of scalable AI-driven tools to manage the surge in patient demand. Additionally, AI-powered platforms like the Zoom extension for telehealth have shown how seamless integration can improve both accessibility and efficiency for healthcare providers.

Our findings show that telehealth providers using SAP solutions have improved patient satisfaction and reduced operational inefficiencies, particularly in areas like appointment scheduling and patient triage. For example, a telehealth company partnered with SAP to integrate ML-driven predictive models, which resulted in a 20% reduction in missed appointments and a more efficient allocation of medical staff.

By integrating these healthcare advancements, SAP demonstrates how machine learning isn’t just about logistics or finance—it’s also transforming lives in critical industries like health.

Machine Learning Algorithms Utilized in SAP Solutions

SAP leverages various machine learning algorithms based on specific use cases.

Algorithm

Use Case

Performance

Regression

Forecasting

Moderate

Clustering

Customer segmentation

High

Neural Networks

Image recognition

Very High

Comparison of Algorithm Performance and Use Cases

Our analysis of SAP’s ML toolkit revealed that:

  • Regression is ideal for linear forecasting (e.g., sales predictions).
  • Clustering excels in grouping customers based on purchasing behavior.
  • Neural Networks outperform other models in complex tasks like fraud detection and image recognition.

Best Practices for Leveraging Machine Learning with SAP

1. Data Preparation and Management

Why It Matters: Garbage in, garbage out. High-quality data is the backbone of successful ML models.

  • Use SAP Data Hub for seamless data integration.
  • Regularly clean and normalize data to improve accuracy.

2. Monitoring and Maintenance of Machine Learning Models

Models degrade over time due to changing business conditions (a concept called model drift).

  • Establish a lifecycle management framework to monitor and retrain models periodically.
  • Use SAP AI Core for automated model deployment and maintenance.

Future Trends in Machine Learning with SAP

The future is bright for machine learning in SAP. Here are some trends to watch:

  1. AI-Powered Process Automation:
    SAP is working on next-gen automation tools to reduce manual workloads further.
  2. Emerging Technologies:
    Tools like SAP Conversational AI are becoming more sophisticated, enabling better natural language understanding.
  3. Edge Computing:
    SAP’s focus on edge computing will enable faster decision-making at localized points of business activity.

Conclusion

Integrating machine learning with SAP solutions is a journey filled with challenges, but the rewards far outweigh the risks. By addressing data quality issues, investing in the right tools, and following best practices, businesses can harness the power of machine learning to revolutionize their operations.

From our team’s perspective, the combination of SAP S/4HANA and machine learning is like having a supercharged engine for business efficiency. Dive in, address the challenges, and let innovation lead the way!

FAQs

  1. What are the main benefits of integrating machine learning with SAP?
    Machine learning enhances decision-making, automates tasks, improves customer experiences, and reduces operational costs.
  2. How does SAP S/4HANA support machine learning?
    SAP S/4HANA includes embedded machine learning capabilities for predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and demand forecasting.
  3. What tools help ensure data quality for ML in SAP?
    Tools like SAP Data Intelligence and SAP Data Hub streamline data integration and cleansing for better ML outcomes.
  4. What industries benefit most from SAP’s ML capabilities?
    Retail, manufacturing, finance, and healthcare are some of the key sectors leveraging SAP’s machine learning tools.
  5. How can businesses overcome skill gaps in ML implementation?
    Invest in training programs through SAP’s online resources or partner with experienced consultants to upskill your team.
  6. What is model drift, and how can it be addressed in SAP?
    Model drift occurs when ML models lose accuracy over time due to changes in data patterns. Regular monitoring and retraining can mitigate this issue.
  7. What are some emerging trends in ML with SAP? AI-powered process automation, conversational AI, and edge computing are shaping the future of ML in SAP solutions.

entry.title: Sydney SAP Stammtisch 2502 (Thurs 27th Feb Shelbourne Hotel)
entry.content: The first Stammtisch of 2025 will be at a new venue, the Shelbourne Hotel Rooftop. We'll be there from 5pm, but feel free to join later on. As usual there is no need to RSVP. NB we'll be on the top (3rd) floor, so you need to take the stairs or lift from the ground floor. There's a retractable roof so we won't be bothered by any rain showers

entry.title: Re: Datasphere Analytic Model oData: 404 Resource Not Found
entry.content:
@TuncayKaraca 

I am also curious about this.

The AM model I am trying to consume via OData contains multiple of the same dimension.
ex) CO_AREA →  0CO_AREA∞D19, 0CO_AREA∞D20, 0CO_AREA∞D21

What does "∞" mean?
How can I check where each dimension is coming from?

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition 2023 FPS2
entry.content:
@andrea_langlotz : 

Hi

Could you please advise whether any enhancement spot available in CPM specially with Issue or Change Request where we can include some customer specific fields.

Since there is no option to include customer specific details (even as a custom screen and storing in the custom table) for CPM objects (master projects, risk, change request) it is becoming a challenge during implementation to map the entire business requirements.

The non-availability of screen enhancement in CPM makes it difficult for implementation. 

Thanks

Abdul

entry.title: Re: BTP application writing to on-premise HANA database t...
entry.content: Hi, It should be easier as it's cloud to cloud so you don't need to care about the firewalls. In DSP, you whitelist the IP (unless it's same platform then most likely you don't need to) and provision a DB user and password of the space with CRUD authorizations (you create one in the space administration) in the njs code where you specify the host/port/uid/pwd. So in the end you just remove some code and change the parameters. Never done it but it's how I would imagine it to work:)

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Hello,

Great blog 😊.

Will it be possible to expose / migrate existing models (new models) to a SAP Datasphere space?

It is not clear for existing models if we are able to do expose, or only for new models (freshly imported or without data).

Many thanks in advance.

entry.title: S/4HANA on-premise extensibility using BTP -- is ABAP environment required?
entry.content:
We have S/4HANA on-premise and we're extending functionality using BTP platform.  There are multiple ways of extending functionality on the BTP platform.  Some options are using the ABAP environment, but so far there has been alternatives options.  We always chose the non-ABAP environment option just to avoid having to add the ABAP environment service to the BTP platform.   But is the incorrect choice?  Would we eventually need to implement the ABAP environment on BTP platform?

What extensibility can only be done in BTP using the ABAP environment?

Thanks,

Regards,

Mel Calucin

entry.title: Re: SAP Callidus
entry.content:
Your understanding is mostly correct! "SAP Callidus" is not a single product but rather a suite of products that SAP acquired from CallidusCloud in 2018.

Clarification on Terminology:

  1. SAP Callidus = SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM) Suite

    • When people say "SAP Callidus", they usually mean the entire suite of sales performance and incentive management solutions that came from CallidusCloud.
    • However, some people incorrectly use "SAP Callidus" specifically to refer to SAP Commissions, since that is the most widely used product.

  2. Product Name Mapping (Before & After SAP Acquisition):

    CallidusCloud Product (Before SAP)                                      SAP Rebranded Name (After Acquisition)
    Callidus TrueComp / CallidusCloud CommissionsSAP Commissions
    Callidus TrueProducer / CallidusCloud Producer ProSAP Agent Lifecycle Management (SAP ALM)
    Callidus CPQSAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote)
    Callidus Territory and QuotaSAP Territory and Quota
    Callidus Litmos (LMS)SAP Litmos (Later sold off, no longer part of SAP)

  3. SAP’s Official Position:

    • SAP does not use the name "SAP Callidus" officially anymore.
    • They refer to it as SAP Sales Cloud or SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM).
    • However, many professionals in the industry still say "SAP Callidus" when they mean SAP Commissions.

Final Answer:

  • "SAP Callidus" originally referred to a suite of products from CallidusCloud.
  • Some people now incorrectly use it to refer only to SAP Commissions.
  • SAP officially refers to these products under SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM).

entry.title: Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor
entry.content:
After last update of SAP Datasphere one of our Analytic Model is break

We receive the message : Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor. Delete and recreate the Analytic Model.

It's impossible to edit the Analytic Model, we can't restore a copy of our JSON file exported before the transport in production

After investigation, we think it is caused due special characters in the technical name

When we try to redeploy the model, we see the message below in the log.

Review the following warnings: Column(s) (/BIC/C_AIE_143) of entity "SPACE001.VG_ISU_DEVICE" contains one or more of (/, \, ", .) character(s) and the same are replaced in the analytical model.

Some fields that are used start by  /BIC/.. these fields are come from BW infoObject or ADSO and worked fine before the update.

Only one solution rebuilt a big Analytic Model 😞

It is therefore noticed that SAP unilaterally changes the functioning of its application without taking into account that there are customers behind the application.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio_How to add two types of time in one task...
entry.content:
Good day Joshuaaa,

Thank you for sharing your inquiry on the SAP Community!  It is recommended that you create a support case using SAP for Me (log in with S-User ID and password) then select the specific SAP Signavio product / component you are referencing.

Kind regards,

KantriceD

entry.title: Re: Create a pareto chart on any model within the SAC
entry.content: Hi, thanks for the simple step by step article. when created this in my system, the "Sort" option was graded out, as probably we have a lot of SKUs, so I ranked them by top 200. but then our graph in the end was not sorted by QTY and Pareto like shown. (top to bottom QTY and small to large in Pareto) any ideas?

entry.title: Enter Manual Contract Accruals
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a question regarding the “ Enter Manual Contract Accruals “ feature in the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents

We are using the Revenue Recognition Methods for Service Orders “ CCOCC and CCOCF ”, and we have a scenario were in a specific period the deferred cost is bigger than the deferred revenue for a single item (Unrealized Costs).

The issue is if we run the Event Base Revenue recognition for this item in the service order, the Income statement will be impacted with loss value for this period, as the system recognized all the deferred values.

Our question, can we use the “ Enter Manual Contract Accruals “ feature to shift recognizing the cost to next period?

 Can we change the account associated in the feature ?

Same for Edge and Firefox.

Re: Auto Booking of Absences for Company Closing Days?

entry.title: Need Help with Error 32 - Broken Pipe, HTTP Adapter - Avoid Sending Response in Certain Cases
entry.content:
Hi Tech Community,

I’m facing the following issue:

A sender using HTTP (which, as we know, is synchronous) sends a payload and expects a response. If the flow crashes, there is no business impact since the sender has a retry mechanism.

The sender side closes the connection after 30 seconds due to business requirements. However, we cannot catch this error because it is not raised during the flow execution but rather occurs outside of it (if I understand correctly how servlets are built). As a result, while the business is not impacted, CPI monitoring detects failures and requires constant checks.

This results in Error 32 - Broken Pipe, as the connection is already closed when the response is attempted to be sent back.

I attempted to implement logic that terminates the flow after 30+ seconds, but the Termination End still sends a response to the sender, causing a failure due to the closed connection.

Does anyone have insights on this issue or ideas on how to prevent sending a response with the HTTP adapter in certain cases?

Looking forward to your responses.

Best regards,
Andrejs

entry.title: Re: Controlling internal movement of an expired Batch and blocked stock type- BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTG
entry.content:
Hi Shailesh,

1) Is it possible to store items with mixed expiry dates in the same bin?  I've noticed the MIX SLED option in the Storage Type configuration, but it appears that different expiry dates cannot be managed as they are either set to the earliest date, the latest date, or not allowed altogether.   

If so, how can this be achieved?                                                                                                                                                    

2) We have multiple serial numbers with different expiration dates. We want to change those dates if they are usable in the future, but we are encountering difficulties.

While there is an option in the warehouse monitor to change attributes in physical stock, it is not effective for serials with different expiration dates within the same bin. This behavior seems to be influenced by the MIX SLED configuration in the storage type.

Could you please advise on how we can change the expiration dates in this scenario?

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Sankara

EWM - Interfaces  EWM - Shipping and Receiving  EWM - Warehouse Order Processing

entry.title: Re: SAP Callidus
entry.content:
Thank you, Kameshwar Nukala, for clearing up the confusion.
I will make sure to use the official product names and inform my fellow consultant colleagues as well.
I was always concerned about these terms when I used to attend interviews, so I just wanted to confirm if my understanding was correct.

entry.title: Bad Request Error while creating SAP BTP Process Integration Runtime instance using Terraform
entry.content:
My Goal - To create and maintain SAP BTP infrastructure like Directory, Subaccounts, Cloud Foundry subscription, Process Integration Runtime instance, Role Collections, Roles, etc for transformation projects using Terraform

What I have accomplished so far - I have managed to use Terraform to create Directory, Subaccounts, Cloud Foundry subscription and assign Process Integration Runtime entitlement to the subaccount, but while creating an instance, its throwing a Bad Request error.

The Code - 

 

 

data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "pir_service_plan" {
  subaccount_id = var.subaccount_id
  name          = btp_subaccount_entitlement.pir_entl.service_name
  offering_name = btp_subaccount_entitlement.pir_entl.plan_name
}
 
resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" "pir" {
  subaccount_id = var.subaccount_id  
  # The service plan ID can be looked up via the data source btp_subaccount_service_plan
  serviceplan_id = "bd5e893f-81dd-4d10-8343-e02975e8ecd8"
  name           = "pir-${var.subaccount_id}"

  parameters = jsonencode({
"roles": [
       <<Role1>>,
       <Role2>>
    ],
    "grant-types": [
        "client_credentials"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [],
    "token-validity": 43200

  })
}

 

 
 

Essentially, I am trying to use the resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" to create an instance of Process Integration Runtime. But for this I need the serviceplan_Id. Which I should be able to get using the data source "btp_subaccount_service_plan"

 

But no matter what data I pass to this data source I get the following error - 

Planning failed. Terraform encountered an error while generating this plan.

Error: API Error Reading Resource Service Plan (Subaccount) with module.process_integration_runtime.data.btp_subaccount_service_plan.service_plans, on modules/process_integration_runtime/process_integration_runtime.tf line 59, in data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "service_ 59: data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "service_plans" { │ │ BadRequest ╵ Operation failed: failed running terraform plan (exit 1) 

 Currently, I am passing "it-rt" as the name parameter and "api" as the offering_name parameter. No matter what value I give, it's always throwing me an error.  

Any help is greatly appreciated

Thanks

Kriba

and sent the screenshot  to digitalskills@sap.com. But, I haven't received the campaign code, as yet?  Any suggestions would be appreciated

entry.title: Re: Why I decided to work through a Beginner Tutorial Mission and what I learned from it
entry.content:
Good idea.  I have been doing this for years and almost always learn s/t new.

Remember, there is no such thing as "on-premise" in real English.
"Premise" is a concept.
"Premises" is a location.

"https://collectivecontent.agency/2018/04/19/should-i-say-on-premise-or-on-premises-it/"

entry.title: S/4HANA planning Cost Center and Allocations cycles
entry.content:
Hello,

I little confused with Planning and also with allocation cycles in S/4HANA.

I know how to plan cost centers with FIORI apps "Import planning data". I also know to create and run allocation with FIORI apps "Manage Allocations". Both apps are updating universal journal tables (ACDOCP & ACDOCA). The old summary tables will not be updated.

I want to do the same (Planning & allocations) but without using FIORI. I want to plan and allocate cost in SAP GUI only and to so by updating universal journal tables (ACDOCP & ACDOCA). I know that for that I cannot use old transaction codes like KP06 or KSV5, KSU5. 

Can someone clarify the options to plan and also allocate cost updating universal journal tables? How can I do so by using GUI only?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Write PCR based on a date condition
entry.content:
Hello Barnes,

Thanks for your cooperation, but I believe that my PCR is already as the one you have suggested

So, do you have any suggestions how to solve that issue.

Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: Not able to add more than one data source in sapui5 a...
entry.content:
I have identified the solution. To resolve the issue, please follow these steps:

  1. Open Application Info.

  2. Navigate to Manage Service Models.

  3. Select the appropriate destination.

  4. Click Refresh & Save.

This process will fix the issue.

Note: You must stop and restart the preview to see the service changes.

entry.title: Fiori & CAP: getting custom action working
entry.content:
Hi,

In a list report on the detail page (object page) , I'm not understanding in general how custom action bound to an entity is to be implemented. I like to update table Content on an object page with an action related to the current displayed entity which is bound to the object page.

The Fiori sdk docu is confusing, the flexible programming odel homepage with section "custom Actions" is not really helpful, does not explain in comments what should be done.

My main entity is SystemKinds, my table on the object page is SystemKinds.texts . I simply like to add a custom action in the toolbar of table SystemKinds.Texts. Because of having bound the object page to SystemKinds, I assume I have to use "bound action". Once the button is pressed, "title" value should be entered by the user.

I also know that I need side effect once the button on a new action is pressed in order to get values in the SystemKinds.texts updated.

Is there a sample which I can check? In the Fiori Show case app, I cannot find my case.
I need the Fiori annotation for the UI and manifest Setup. I tried different things but nothing works.
I need a short advice in Fiori Setup (annotations with UI) . The action is "setSystemKindsTitles".

Backend Looks like:

 

 

 entity SystemKinds as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds
    actions {      
      @(Common.SideEffects: {TargetEntities: ['in/texts']})
      action setSystemKindsTitles(
                                  @(                                   UI.Placeholder:'{i18n>Paltr_Common_InputTitlePlaceHolder}',
                                    title:'{i18n>Paltr_Common_InputTitle}'
                                  )
                                  title : String);


    };

  // --- SystemKinds.texts ---
  @Capabilities: {
    Insertable            : false,
    Deletable             : false,
    Updatable,
    NavigationRestrictions: {Navigability: #None, }
  }
  entity SystemKinds.texts as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds.texts;

 

 

In my Service,I have implemented:

 

 

...
class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {

   const { SystemTypes, SystemKinds } = this.entities;

if (SystemKinds.texts) {
      this.on('setSystemKindsTitles', SystemKinds, async (req) => {
        if (!!SystemKinds.texts?.drafts) {
          await this.update(SystemKinds.texts.drafts, req.data.ID).with({ title: req.data.title });
        }

      });
    }
   ...
}

 

 

My currently used Fiori UI looks like the following:

 

 

using AdminConfigurationService as service from '../../srv/paltr.service.core.adminconfiguration';

annotate service.SystemKinds with @(
    // Listreport SystemKindList

    UI.LineItem                                          : [
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: title,
        },
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isPreset,
        },
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isUsable,
        },
    ],

    // SystemKindObjectPage
    UI.HeaderInfo                                        : {
        TypeName      : '{i18n>Paltr_SystemKind}',
        TypeNamePlural: '{i18n>Paltr_SystemKinds}',
        Title         : {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : techName,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
            ![@UI.Hidden]    : false,

        },


    },

    // SystemKindObjectPage - section General Information
    UI.FieldGroup #Paltr_GeneralInformationFieldGroup    : {
        $Type: 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: techName,
            },
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: ID,
            },
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAnnotation',
                Target: '@UI.ConnectedFields#Paltr_GeneralInformationConnected',
                Label : '{i18n>Paltr_LastChange}',
            },
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: isPreset,
            },
        ],
    },
    UI.ConnectedFields #Paltr_GeneralInformationConnected: {
        $Type   : 'UI.ConnectedFieldsType',
        Template: '{modifiedBy}{modifiedAt}',
        Data    : {
            $Type     : 'Core.Dictionary',
            modifiedBy: {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: modifiedBy,
            },
            modifiedAt: {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: modifiedAt,
            },
        },
    },

    // SystemKindObjectPage - section Settings
    UI.FieldGroup #Paltr_SettingsFieldGroup              : {
        $Type: 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [

        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isUsable,
        }],

    },


    UI.Facets                                            : [
        //Section General Information
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            ID    : 'SystemKind_GeneralInformationFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_GeneralInformation}',
            Target: '@UI.FieldGroup#Paltr_GeneralInformationFieldGroup',
        },

        //Section Settings
        {
            $Type : 'UI.CollectionFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_BaseData}',
            ID    : 'SystemKind_SettingsFacet',
            Facets: [{
                $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
                Label : '{i18n>Paltr_Settings}',
                ID    : 'SystemKind_SettingsGroupFacet',
                Target: '@UI.FieldGroup#Paltr_SettingsFieldGroup',
            }],
        },

        // Translations table
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_Translations}',
            ID    : 'Paltr_Translations',
            Target: 'texts/@UI.LineItem#Paltr_Translations',

        },

   
    ],


);

annotate service.SystemKinds.texts with @(UI.LineItem #Paltr_Translations: [
    {
        $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
        Value            : locale,
        Label            : '{i18n>Paltr_Sprache}',
        ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
    },
    {
        $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
        Value            : title,
        ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
    },

    
]);

 

 

The new action shoud appear in the toolbar of "Translations table". I have deleted all my own trials around actions.

Any help would be great. 

Yours

Stephan

entry.title: Post good receipt BADI on Public cloud - Addition of Batch and characteristic in Custom fields
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a scenario where i need to display some extra fields in the APP- Material document overview. I need to display batch and characteristics details in the item page. The std fields are not available in the adapt UI mode.

We tried to create 2 custom fields for the same and added it in Adapt UI. The fields are coming now but I am not able to fill values into in during the runtime since its all CDS views and SEGW odata service. I dont have any point to fill it during runtime during display of items.

So to fill the two custom fields We tried to implement the BADI -MMIM_MODIFY_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_ITEM  which gets called from Post good receipt app. The user enters the PO and batch and characteristics details in the screen whcih we need to show on the Material document overview APP.

But this BADI does not have any parameters which can read the values from the screen. It only gives header and line item data so we are not able to update the custom field values. This is leading blank value in the report- Material document overview for those custom fields.

 

Is there any badi to achieve this requirement ?

Or is there anything that can be done on the material document overview app to display extra std fields related to batch via extensibility ?

 

Material document overview - Item data

 

 

In Migo user enters the batch details

Doing a select on the CDS does not help since the data is not yet saved.

 

Now the only option left is to create a new Material document overview app with custom CDS views and bring those value as extra columns using logic. But i see this solution as very time consuming for such a small ask.

 

Note : This is to be done on S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD.

 

Any other approach/help/pointers/guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP - How to identify the hostname?
entry.content:
Hi @oliverschultze ,

To ensure the integration works, you first need to create an SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 tenant, with each tenant representing a business unit in your CDP.
Once the tenants are created, one of the step is to set up the Communication System to enable data exchange with CDP.
When creating the Communication System, use the host name from the URL (e.g., https://cdp.Eu5-prod.gigya.com), the OAuth token URL, and your Client Credentials from the CDP event.
For further details, refer to Communication Systems in the documentation

Thanks,
Anand Raj

entry.title: Re: WF Lockbox Not Posted Completely For 12/30/2024
entry.content:
Hi,

 

you have to post them in the Fiori App named Reprocess Lockbox Items.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: Lockbox Issue-Unprocessed Status-Message FA 006 - Pay...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

yeah you can open the lockbox item in the Reprocess Lockbox Items app and then simple delete the payment advice ID which is entered there.

Then you can go ahead and post or clear invoices as you wish.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: SAP CPQ Integration with Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM)
entry.content:
Background

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) sell side adapter for SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates the contracting flow to the sell side process. New quotes created on SAP CPQ are automatically transitioned into a Contract Request on Sirion CLM enabling a sync between two systems for the entire sales and contracting flow.

The package contains:

1. Integration flows for:
    a) Initiation of Contract Draft Requests based on SAP CPQ quotes including line items
    b) Tracking the contract request status and enabling quick access to the Contract on Sirion CLM

2. Configuration guides for SAP CPQ, SAP Cloud Integration, Sirion CLM and Integration flow are available on the Documents tab.

The adapter can be configured by defining integration points, mapping data fields between SAP CPQ and Sirion CLM, setting up Sirion API authentication on SAP Cloud Integration.  

Note : This will be partner based Integration packages offered and supports for SAP CPQ.

Integration Package - SAP API Business Hub

SAP CPQ - CLM Integration Document

entry.title: Re: How Automated Processing Rules for Bank Statement works?
entry.content: Hi @Aswathi_Menon, great blog post!

entry.title: SAP BTP Process Automation
entry.content:
Greetings, 

I'm working on creating a from in SAP BTP Process Automation.

My understanding is that when capturing data from a form, it is stored as part of the process instance's context, and it is available to all subsequent steps within the same workflow. The context data persists for the lifetime of the process instance. Once the process is completed, the data will be completely gone and not accessible anymore, correct? 

If that is not true or correct, how can I fetch or trace them?

entry.title: note 3304656 : E:TN:872 SAPK61700QCPSAPAPPL
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

When i trying to implement the SNOTE 3304656 a message error occured : E:TN:872 SAPK61700QCPSAPAPPL , the current version of SAP_APPL version: 617-SP LEVEL: 0008 and ECC-DIMP version 600 - SP LEVEL: 0026.

Thanks for help in advance

best regards.

entry.title: Re: Step-By-Step Guide: Create trial account for SAP Integration Suite(2025) & Build an HTTPS iF
entry.content: @Lalitha_K Thank you.
